Simon Says Game

Classic memory pattern game. Watch the colored buttons light up, then repeat the sequence. How long can you remember?

Watch the sequence...
1Level
1Length
1Best

Complete the game to see your score!

How to Play Simon Says

1

Watch the colored buttons light up in sequence.

2

Click the buttons in the exact same order they lit up.

3

Each correct round adds one more step to the sequence.

4

One wrong click ends the game. Your score is the level you reached.

Tips to Improve Your Score

  • Create a mental rhythm or melody with the colors
  • Assign numbers or names to each color for easier recall
  • Watch the entire sequence before trying to repeat it
  • Focus on accuracy â€” one wrong press ends the game

Frequently Asked Questions

What is Simon Says?

Simon Says is a classic electronic memory game invented in 1978. Four colored buttons light up in a sequence that grows longer with each round. The player must repeat the sequence exactly. It's a test of sequential memory and attention.

What's a good score?

Reaching level 8-10 is good for beginners. Level 15+ is above average. Level 20+ is excellent. The world record for the original Simon game exceeds 30 levels.

About the Simon Says

The Simon Says is one of our most popular brain training exercises. Designed to test and improve your cognitive abilities, this game provides instant feedback on your performance.

Regular practice with this type of cognitive exercise can help improve your mental processing speed, accuracy, and overall brain health. We recommend practicing for 10-15 minutes daily for the best results.

Your scores are saved locally on your device, allowing you to track your progress over time. Challenge yourself to beat your personal best with each attempt!
